Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive light-verb idiom; body-part noun + postpositional phrase + causative/agentive verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To deceive someone by hiding the truth or creating a false impression.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: किसी के धोखा देना या बात लुकाना.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech; bargaining; family disputes; workplace excuses; political criticism.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in agrarian life where dust in the eyes blocks clear seeing; common metaphor for deception in Chota Nagpur speech communities.
First Example Sentence: दलाल हमर आँखि में धूर झोंक के खराब जमीन बेच देलक.
Second Example Sentence: मीटिंग में ऊ आँकड़ा बदल के सभे कर आँखि में धूर झोंकय चाहत रहे.
Third Example Sentence: साँच बात कह, मोर आँखि में धूर झोंकय कर कोसिस मत कर.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed adverbial clause; body-part noun + instrumental/postpositional phrase + conjunctive participle + intransitive verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To ignore warnings, advice, or urgent matters deliberately.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: जरूरी बात के अनसुना कर देना.
Usage Contexts: Family advice; public warnings; school discipline; village meetings.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with everyday household speech: oil in the ear symbolically prevents hearing, so the idiom mocks stubborn inattention.
First Example Sentence: बरखा से पहिले छप्पर बनाय कहली, मुदा ऊ कान में तेल देके सुतल रहल.
Second Example Sentence: गाँव कर बुजुर्ग मन चेतावनी देलथिन, फेर जवान मन कान में तेल देके सुत गेलथें.
Third Example Sentence: डॉक्टर कर बात कान में तेल देके मत सुत, दवाई बखत पर खा.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Causative verb phrase; body-part noun + causative predicate expressing social consequence.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To bring shame, dishonor, or public embarrassment to oneself, family, or group.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: परिवार या समूह के लाज घटा देना.
Usage Contexts: Family honor; school conduct; community reputation; public criticism.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects the strong social value placed on reputation and collective honor in village and kinship networks.
First Example Sentence: झूठ पकड़ा गेलक तें पूरा टोली कर नाक कटाय देलक.
Second Example Sentence: परीक्षा में नकल करबऽ तें अपन घर कर नाक कटाय देबऽ.
Third Example Sentence: अधिकारि कर खराब बरताव से दफ्तर कर नाक कटाय गेलक.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed metaphorical clause; body-part noun + locative phrase + noun object + causative/reflexive light verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To remain silent when one should speak, explain, or defend the truth.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: जरूरी समय पर चुप रह जाना.
Usage Contexts: Arguments; classroom questioning; workplace accountability; sarcasm.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws from food imagery; thick curd in the mouth humorously suggests speech has become blocked.
First Example Sentence: तोरा सब पता रहे, फेर पंचायत में मुँह में दही जमाय लेले रहिस.
Second Example Sentence: मास्टर सवाल पूछलथिन तें छात्र मुँह में दही जमाय लेलक.
Third Example Sentence: गलती ओकर रहे, मुदा ऊ मुँह में दही जमाय के खड़ा रहल.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Locative metaphor; numeral + noun + postpositional phrase + body-part object + transitive verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To try to support two opposing sides or pursue two incompatible choices at once.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: एके समय दू विरोधी रास्ता अपनाना.
Usage Contexts: Politics; business partnerships; family conflict; decision-making.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to riverine and rural travel imagery: standing with feet in two boats is unstable and risky.
First Example Sentence: चुनाव में दू नाव में गोड़ राखबऽ तें जनता भरोसा नइ करबय.
Second Example Sentence: नौकरी आउ कारोबार, दूनो में बिना योजना गोड़ राखबऽ तें मुश्किल होबय.
Third Example Sentence: ऊ हर झगड़ा में दू नाव में गोड़ राखे लगय, सेले कोनो पक्ष ओकर बात नइ माने.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb-object metaphor; noun + locative phrase + object noun + transitive verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To intensify an argument, anger, crisis, or conflict by adding provocative words or actions.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: झगड़ा या समस्या के अउर बढ़ा देना.
Usage Contexts: Family quarrels; politics; social media; workplace conflict.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Based on cooking and ritual fire knowledge: ghee makes flame rise, so it symbolizes escalation.
First Example Sentence: दूनो भाई पहिलहीं गरम रहथें, तें बीच में आगि में घी मत डाल.
Second Example Sentence: अफवाह फैलाय के ओकरा गाँव में आगि में घी डाल देलक.
Third Example Sentence: बैठक शांत होवत रहे, मुदा नेता कर बयान आगि में घी डाल देलक.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Compound noun phrase + resultative light verb construction; hyperbolic idiom.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To work extremely hard with physical, emotional, or long-term effort.
Usage Contexts: Farming; study; migration work; entrepreneurship; sports.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects labor-intensive rural and working-class experience where bodily effort is central to survival and dignity.
First Example Sentence: बाबूजी हमर पढ़ाई खातिर खून-पसीना एक कर देलथिन.
Second Example Sentence: धान कर खेत में किसान मन खून-पसीना एक करथें.
Third Example Sentence: दुकान खड़ा करे में ऊ पाँच बरिस खून-पसीना एक करलक.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Experiencer construction; body-part noun + locative phrase + animal subject + intransitive verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To feel very hungry, often with a playful or exaggerated tone.
Usage Contexts: Home; school; travel; casual conversation; humor with children.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses a vivid household animal image; hunger is imagined as small creatures jumping inside the stomach.
First Example Sentence: सुबह से कुछु नइ खाय हिअइ, मोर पेट में चूहा कूदे लगलक.
Second Example Sentence: बस देरी हो गेलक तें लइका मन कहे लगलथें, पेट में चूहा कूद रहलय.
Third Example Sentence: पहिले खाना दे, फेर बात करब; पेट में चूहा कूदत हे.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Aspectual participial phrase; body-part noun + progressive participle + continuative intransitive verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be left regretting after losing a chance or being unable to act.
Usage Contexts: Business; exams; farming decisions; legal matters; life advice.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Hand-rubbing is a visible gesture of helpless regret in local oral storytelling and everyday reprimand.
First Example Sentence: समय पर आवेदन नइ भरलऽ, आब हाथ मलते रहि जा.
Second Example Sentence: बाजार भाव अच्छा रहे, बेच देतिस तें हाथ मलते नइ रहितिस.
Third Example Sentence: साँच गवाही देवे कर मौका चूक गेलक, आब ऊ हाथ मलते रहि गेलक.

Idiom (Nagpuri): बिल्ली कर गला में घंटी बाँधना
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominalized proverb-like idiom; animal possessor phrase + locative body-part phrase + object + infinitive/transitive verb.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To take the risky first step that everyone discusses but nobody wants to do.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: कठिन और जोखिम वाला काम शुरू करना.
Usage Contexts: Leadership; workplace reform; politics; activism; village councils.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Widely resonant animal-fable motif adapted in Nagpuri oral speech to discuss courage, collective fear, and responsibility.
First Example Sentence: भ्रष्टाचार कर बात सभे करय, मुदा बिल्ली कर गला में घंटी बाँधे के हिम्मत केकरो नइ होय.
Second Example Sentence: कारखाना में सुरक्षा मुद्दा उठाय के माया बिल्ली कर गला में घंटी बाँध देलक.
Third Example Sentence: गाँव कर सड़क खातिर आवेदन देके मुखिया सचमुच बिल्ली कर गला में घंटी बाँधलथिन.
